chief_complaint: A 70-year-old man presents with anorexia two weeks after receiving the third dose of the mRNA-1273 COVID-19 vaccine.
history: The patient has a history of dyslipidemia. Two weeks after vaccination, he developed anorexia, followed by marked elevation of serum creatinine from 0.9 to 2.76 mg/dL, accompanied by proteinuria and elevated tubular injury markers. He also developed bilateral ocular pain and was diagnosed with bilateral uveitis.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Laboratory evaluation showed marked elevation of serum creatinine, proteinuria, and elevated tubular injury markers. Renal biopsy confirmed tubulointerstitial nephritis.
